title: shadows of mordor
body:
culturally speaking my white father raped my black mother
then took me in but could not bother to stop beating people of her color
where i could see it and it darkened my whole perspective
or rather the layers of cover i stood under offered shades
that i accepted even though those lenses ain't corrective
they make it harder for me to navigate the collective
i accept as the bedrock of the private reality i contend with
they said black was of the devil and the only right way to touch it
was from a position of absolute authority that couldn't be corrupted
like god before the rebellion made humanity a subject
like the king before the ism's ousted royalty on the pretext
of freedom even though that isn't even close to describing
the subtext of the history they make us read in class warfare 101
with a
